## Theme Customization

An active Pro subscription allows you to change the look of Raycast, and you can use the Theme Studio to pick a pre-defined theme or create your own.

## Color System

The colors automatically adapt to the Raycast theme (light or dark). A dynamic color applies different colors depending on the active Raycast theme, and when using a Dynamic Color, it will be adjusted to achieve high contrast with the Raycast user interface.

Raycast supports multiple color formats including "Short HEX, RGBA, RGBA Percentage, HSL, and Keywords" for specifying colors in extensions.

Raycast has been added as a Destination to Specify, which allows you to search and consume all your design tokens and assets from your computer in seconds.

There's a tool to easily share, browse and import Raycast Themes. Additionally, you can generate a Raycast Theme from an image such as your wallpaper.

Based on the search results, here's an overview of Raycast's UI design and command palette interface:

## UI Design Architecture

Raycast uses React for its user interface declaration and renders the supported elements to our native UI. The API comes with a set of UI components that you can use to build your extensions.

## Command Palette Interface Design

Raycast's primary interface is a command palette invoked by a hotkey (default Alt+Space, customizable). Type a query and you can invoke actions, with this approach emphasizing minimal UI, fast fuzzy search, and immediate actions instead of a multi-pane discovery surface.

## Recent UI Refinements

Raycast simplified the user interface and consolidated actions, toasts, and navigation titles in an all-new action bar at the bottom of the window. The left part of the bar shows the navigation title of the active command and any toast, while the right part of the bar shows actions available in the current context, along with their keyboard shortcut, letting users learn action shortcuts to make using Raycast even faster.

## Advanced Command Palette Features

Raycast features advanced command palette features such as "Palettes in Palettes," demonstrating the versatility of this interface pattern and its ability to accommodate increasingly complex functionality.

## Compact Mode

With Compact Mode, Raycast's user interface is more focused. The new mode optimizes the typical flow of opening Raycast and typing in what you're looking for, blending all other elements for a minimal appearance, and can be enabled in Raycast's preferences to bring more focus to your work.

## Action Panel UI Component

Each component in Raycast can provide interaction via an ActionPanel, which has a list of Actions where each one can be associated with a keyboard shortcut. Shortcuts allow users to use Raycast without using their mouse.

## Default Keyboard Shortcuts

In List, Grid, and Detail views, the primary action is assigned ↵ (Enter) and the secondary action is ⌘ ↵, while in Form views it's ⌘ ↵ for primary and ⌘ ⇧ ↵ for secondary.

## Bottom Bar Design

Raycast consolidated actions, toasts, and navigation titles in an all-new action bar at the bottom of the window. The bar has two sections: the left part shows the navigation title of the active command and any toast, while the right part of the bar shows actions available in the current context, along with their keyboard shortcut, which lets users learn action shortcuts to make using Raycast even faster.

## Keyboard Shortcut Definition

A keyboard shortcut is defined by one or more modifier keys (command, control, etc.) and a single key equivalent (a character or special key).

# Raycast UI Components Overview

Based on the provided documentation, here are the UI components Raycast offers:

## High-Level Components

**1. List**
- Purpose: "to show multiple similar items, f.e. a list of your open todos"
- No specific visual details provided in the source

**2. Grid**
- Purpose: "similar to a List but with more legroom to show an image for each item"
- Example use case: "a collection of icons"
- Visual distinction: offers more space compared to List for displaying images

**3. Detail**
- Purpose: "to present more information, f.e. the details of a GitHub pull request"
- Designed for displaying comprehensive content

**4. Form**
- Purpose: "to create new content, f.e. filing a bug report"
- Used for user input and content creation

## Interactive Element

**ActionPanel**
- Contains a list of Actions
- Each Action can be associated with keyboard shortcuts
- Enables mouse-free interaction with Raycast

## Component Properties

The documentation mentions an `isLoading` property available on top-level components (Detail, Form, List) that displays "a loading indicator at the top of Raycast" when set to true.

**Note:** The source material does not provide detailed specifications regarding colors, typography, spacing, or other visual design system details.

# Raycast Design System Visual Details

Based on the blog post, here are the key visual design elements:

## Search Bar & Icons
The search bar received a **larger size** to emphasize its central role. Icon sizes were also **increased** for faster visual scanning of search results.

## Action Bar
A new bottom-positioned action bar consolidates actions, toasts, and navigation. The **left section** displays navigation titles and toast notifications, while the **right section** shows available actions with keyboard shortcuts displayed inline.

## Icon Set
Collaborated with designer James McDonald on a complete icon overhaul using:
- Outline style with **consistent stroke width**
- **Uniform corner radii** applied across all icons
- "Balanced set of icons that feel fresh and new"

## Compact Mode
Optimizes the typical search-and-execute workflow by **blending other UI elements** for minimal visual presence while maintaining the search interface.

## App Icon
The new icon uses a **keycap metaphor** with **three-dimensional appearance**, inspired by Apple's Magic Keyboard design, featuring a rounded-rectangle shape aligned with macOS Big Sur conventions.

## Design Principles
The three core principles guiding all changes: "fast, simple, and delightful," applied throughout from major features to micro-interactions.

**Note:** The article contains no specific pixel values, hex color codes, or CSS token definitions.

# Raycast Action Panel Details

## Overview
The Action Panel is described as "one of the most important features of Raycast" that enables users to "find and take any action in the app with just a few keystrokes."

## Key Keyboard Shortcuts

The documentation highlights three primary keyboard interactions:

- **`↵` (Return)**: Executes the primary action in lists. The system adapts contextually—whether launching applications, opening commands, or displaying GitHub pull request details.

- **`⌘` `↵` (Command+Return)**: Submits form inputs when working within forms.

- **`⌘` `K` (Command+K)**: Displays additional available actions and reveals their corresponding keyboard shortcuts for faster future access.

## Functional Characteristics

The interface demonstrates context-aware behavior, where the primary action adjusts based on the user's current location within the application. This design pattern encourages muscle memory development, as users can consistently rely on the Return key to perform the most relevant action.

## Visual Implementation

The page includes an image demonstrating the Action Panel in a terminal-style interface, though specific visual details about layout, grouping, or sectioning are not explicitly described in the extracted content.

# Color System Extraction

## Standard Color Palette
The documentation lists nine enumeration members:
- **Hue Colors**: Blue, Green, Magenta, Orange, Purple, Red, Yellow
- **Text Colors**: PrimaryText, SecondaryText

Each color automatically adapts between light and dark themes, though specific hex values aren't provided in the documentation.

## Supported Color Formats

The system accepts multiple color specification methods:

**Hex Formats**: Standard six-digit hex (`#FF0000`) and short three-digit hex (`#F00`)

**RGB/RGBA**: Full syntax like `rgb(255, 0, 0)` and percentage-based `rgb(255, 0, 0, 1.0)`

**HSL/HSLA**: Example given as `hsla(200, 20%, 33%, 0.2)`

**CSS Keywords**: Named colors such as `red`

## Color Type System

The API supports three approaches:

1. **Built-in Colors** (e.g., `Color.Red`) — theme-aware with automatic contrast adjustment
2. **Dynamic Colors** — separate light/dark specifications with optional `adjustContrast` boolean property
3. **Raw Colors** — direct string values, automatically adjusted for contrast unless disabled

The documentation emphasizes that "we recommend leaving color adjustment on, unless your extension depends on exact color reproduction."

# ActionPanel Reference Summary

## Core Structure

The ActionPanel exposes "a list of actions that can be performed by the user," functioning as context-aware controls within Raycast's UI components.

## Primary Components

**ActionPanel**: The main container with optional title display. It accepts sections or actions as children, automatically creating a default section if only actions are specified.

**ActionPanel.Section**: Groups related actions visually. Useful for organizing numerous actions—for example, grouping copy operations or dangerous actions separately.

**ActionPanel.Submenu**: Replaces the current panel with submenu children when selected, enabling hierarchical action selection (like adding labels or assignees).

## Keyboard Shortcuts & Visual Hierarchy

The first and second actions become primary and secondary actions:
- **List/Grid/Detail**: Primary uses `↵`, secondary uses `⌘↵`
- **Form**: Primary uses `⌘↵`, secondary uses `⌘⇧↵`

Custom shortcuts can be assigned to submenu items via the `shortcut` prop, though primary/secondary shortcuts won't display custom assignments.

## Key Props

| Component | Notable Props |
|-----------|--------------|
| ActionPanel | `title`, `children` |
| ActionPanel.Section | `title`, `children` |
| ActionPanel.Submenu | `title` (required), `icon`, `autoFocus`, `onOpen`, `onSearchTextChange`, `filtering`, `isLoading`, `shortcut`, `throttle` |

## Advanced Features

The Submenu supports lazy loading via the `onOpen` callback and includes filtering capabilities with search text change handling and throttling options for performance optimization.

# Comprehensive List Component Overview

## Core Visual Structure

The List component serves as a foundational UI element displaying organized content with built-in search, filtering, and detail capabilities. Its architecture comprises several interconnected parts:

### Main Container
The `<List>` wrapper displays items and sections with properties including:
- **Navigation and Search**: `navigationTitle`, `searchBarPlaceholder`, `searchText`
- **State Management**: `isLoading`, `isShowingDetail`, `selectedItemId`
- **Filtering**: Native fuzzy matching on titles/keywords via `filtering` prop
- **Callbacks**: `onSearchTextChange`, `onSelectionChange`
- **Accessories**: Right-side dropdown via `searchBarAccessory`
- **Pagination**: Optional infinite scroll with `pagination` object

## Content Organization

### List.Section
Groups related items hierarchically with:
- `title`: Section header text
- `subtitle`: Optional descriptive text
- `children`: Contained List.Item elements

### List.Item
Individual content units featuring:
- **Primary Content**: `title` (required), `subtitle`, `icon`
- **Identification**: Unique `id` (auto-generated if omitted)
- **Searchability**: `keywords` array for extended indexing
- **Actions**: Attached `ActionPanel` for user interactions
- **Accessories**: Right-aligned metadata display
- **Details**: Optional `detail` panel for expanded content
- **Quick Look**: `quickLook` property for file previews

## Visual Metadata System

### List.Item.Accessory
Right-aligned display elements showing:
- `text` or `tag`: Label information (string, Date, or colored variants)
- `date`: Relative time formatting (e.g., "now," "1d")
- `icon`: Visual indicator
- `tooltip`: Hover information

**Example accessory types**: "An Accessory Text," colored text with icon, date stamps, tagged values

### List.Item.Detail.Metadata
Structured bottom-panel information containing:

**Metadata.Label**: Title + optional icon + optional text
**Metadata.Link**: Navigation target with associated text
**Metadata.TagList**: Grouped tag displays with individual colors
**Metadata.Separator**: Visual divider for grouping

## Detail Panel (Right Sidebar)

Activated via `isShowingDetail` prop, displays:
- **Markdown Content**: Rendered CommonMark with embedded images
- **Metadata Section**: Structured data below markdown
- **Loading State**: `isLoading` indicator

## Search & Filter Features

### Search Bar Integration
- Fuzzy matches against item `title` and `keywords`
- Programmatic text control via `searchText` prop
- Custom filtering by setting `filtering={false}`
- Optional throttling for async operations

### Dropdown Accessory
Provides secondary filtering dimension:
- `List.Dropdown` with sections and items
- Keyboard trigger: `⌘P`
- Value persistence via `storeValue`
- Independent `onChange` callbacks

## Empty States

### List.EmptyView
Displays when no items match or list is empty:
- `title`: Main message
- `description`: Explanatory text
- `icon`: Centered visual (accepts image URLs)
- `actions`: Optional ActionPanel

The component note: "Raycast provides a default EmptyView" if none specified, and it won't display while `isLoading` is true.

## Pagination System

Requires `@raycast/api` v1.69.0+:
- `onLoadMore`: Async callback triggered at list bottom
- `hasMore`: Boolean flag enabling further loading
- `pageSize`: Placeholder count during loading

**Memory protection**: Raycast monitors extension memory; pagination halts automatically if exhaustion risk detected.

## Key Behavioral Props

| Property | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `filtering` | Toggle/customize native search matching; string/Date filtering |
| `throttle` | Delay `onSearchTextChange` for intensive operations |
| `selectedItemId` | Programmatically select specific item |
| `onSelectionChange` | Track user navigation (receives null when all filtered) |

## Complete Example Interaction Flow

A typical List displays beer names in sections (Lager/IPA), shows details on selection, includes colored accessories (origin country), offers a dropdown filter by type, and triggers actions (selection confirmation) via ActionPanel attached to each item.

# Raycast Theme Explorer Analysis

Based on the GitHub repository page provided, I cannot extract the actual theme color tokens, hex values, or specific color configurations you're requesting.

## What's Available

The page shows this is a **Next.js 13 application** for sharing and managing Raycast themes, but the actual theme data files aren't displayed in the provided content. The repository structure indicates themes are stored in JSON format within the `/themes` directory, organized by username (e.g., `/themes/peduarte/red.json`).

## Key Process Mentioned

According to the documentation: "Right click on your Theme and select 'Copy as JSON'" from Raycast's Theme Studio, then contributors "paste the theme JSON you copied from Raycast's Theme Studio" into the repository.

## Missing Information

The actual **color token definitions, palette structures, hex values, and default colors** (background, text, selection, accent) are not included in this README or repository overview. To access this data, you would need to:

1. Visit the actual theme files in the `/themes` directory
2. Examine individual JSON files like `peduarte/red.json`
3. Check the theme generation scripts that create OG images (`npm run generate-og-images`)

The page confirms themes exist but doesn't expose their internal color specifications in the displayed content.

# Raycast Theme Color Tokens

## Core Color Variables

The Raycast manual documents an extensive theming system with color tokens organized by hue. Here are the primary customizable theme properties:

### Primary Color Families

**Gray Scale**: `--gray-h`, `--gray-s`, `--gray-l` with derived tokens for text, backgrounds, pills, and form buttons

**Brown Palette**: `--brown-h`, `--brown-s`, `--brown-l` generating text colors, backgrounds (standard and light variants), pill styling, and button fills

**Orange/Yellow Range**: Orange and yellow tokens follow identical structures with hue values around 25-38 degrees

**Green/Blue/Purple**: `--green-h` (146°), `--blue-h` (217°), `--purple-h` (270°) each with saturation and lightness variants

**Pink/Red**: `--pink-h` (329°), `--red-h` (1°) completing the spectrum

### Derived Token Categories

Each primary color generates:
- Text color (`--color-text-[name]`)
- Background variants (`--color-bg-[name]`, `--color-bg-[name]-light`)
- Pill styling (`--color-pill-[name]`, `--color-pill-text-[name]`)
- Form button backgrounds (`--color-bg-form-btn-[name]`)

### UI Component Tokens

**Navigation**: `--navbar-text-color`, `--navbar-background-color`, `--navbar-button-text-color`, button hover states

**Sidebar**: Text, CTA text, backgrounds, borders, and hover states

**Scrollbars**: `--scrollbar-background-color`, `--scrollbar-thumb-color`, `--scrollbar-border-color`

**General**: Default text colors, border colors, UI hover backgrounds, card backgrounds, checkbox background

Dark mode example hex: `#191919` (default background), `#e1e1e1` (text)

# Raycast Preferences/Settings Overview

Based on the web page content, here's a comprehensive breakdown of the Raycast preferences window structure:

## Access Methods
Users can open preferences by clicking the Raycast icon in the menu bar or using the keyboard shortcut `⌘` `,` when Raycast is active. A pro tip mentions searching "preferences" in the root search for quick access.

## Preferences Tabs

**General Tab**
The General section allows customization of core functionality. The documentation recommends launching Raycast at login and replacing Apple's Spotlight. This approach means users "only need to remember one hotkey" and maintain constant access to Raycast.

**Appearance Tab**
This tab offers theme selection with support for both light and dark modes. The default setting adopts the system appearance, though users can manually select either option. Additionally, there's a text size toggle between the default option and a slightly larger variant.

**Extensions Tab**
This section manages all available extensions with toggles for enabling/disabling individual extensions and their commands. Some extensions include additional customizations accessible through the right panel. Examples include Jira login capabilities and signature settings for new issues. Users can also configure aliases and hotkeys here to enhance productivity.

**Advanced Tab**
The Advanced settings handle additional configuration options including display positioning, timeout settings before returning to root search, and more. Notably, this tab offers export/import functionality for preferences, allowing users to transfer settings to new devices without reinstalling extensions or reconfiguring commands and hotkeys.

**About Tab**
This tab provides version information, links to the official website, feedback options, and acknowledgements.

## AI Chat Window Interface

AI Chat opens in a resizable, floating window by default. Recent design updates include Liquid Glass controls in AI Chat, reflecting modern macOS design trends.

## Input and Markdown Code Blocks

Code blocks in AI Chat now have a "copy" button in the top-right corner, and markdown code blocks support syntax highlighting. Recent improvements include significantly improved performance when loading large code blocks and the ability to copy code blocks during completion.

# Raycast AI Chat Visual Details

Based on the manual content provided, here's what's documented about the AI Chat interface:

## Core Layout
The AI Chat is described as "a standalone chat window to ask AI anything," functioning as an interactive conversational interface within Raycast.

## Interaction Elements
- **Input mechanism**: Users type questions to initiate and continue conversations
- **Follow-up capability**: The system supports continuous dialogue with follow-up questions
- **Command integration**: Users can press `⌘ K` to access the command menu and apply AI Commands (like "Make Shorter") to previous messages

## Message Display
Messages appear in a conversational format within the chat window, though specific styling details aren't elaborated in the documentation.

## Controls & Features
- **Keyboard shortcut**: Assignable global hotkey (example: `⌥ J`) for quick access
- **Settings location**: Preferences → Extensions → Raycast AI → AI Chat
- **Chat branching**: Experimental feature allowing alternate conversation paths via `CMD + Shift + B`
- **Attachments**: Users can provide additional context through file attachments
- **Feedback system**: Thumbs up/down buttons appear when hovering over messages

## Model Selection
Users can select different AI models through an integrated model picker within the chat interface.

The documentation emphasizes conversational continuity rather than detailed UI specifications.

# Raycast Store Interface Overview

## Core Functionality
The Store serves as Raycast's central extension marketplace. Users must agree to terms and conditions upon first opening the Store command before accessing the extension catalog.

## Search & Discovery
Extensions are indexed by title and author-provided keywords. The interface includes a search field at the top of the Store Command for keyword-based lookups. Users can alternatively browse the web-based store at raycast.com/store to view extension details, developer profiles, and initiate installations that redirect to the Raycast app.

## Extension Display
Extensions are presented with multiple interaction options:
- **Primary action**: Pressing Enter installs new extensions
- **Installed extensions**: Show "Open Command" as the primary action, optionally displaying multiple commands within an extension
- **Details view**: Accessed via "Show Details" button, revealing available commands, developer information, and metadata

## Installation Flow
From the web store, users encounter an interstitial screen within the Raycast app. They can press Enter or click "Install Extension" to proceed. The documentation emphasizes keeping Raycast updated, as outdated versions may prevent proper extension functionality or installation.

## Uninstall Process
Extensions can be removed through Settings or by selecting "Uninstall Extension" from the action panel when viewing extension commands.

## Configuration
Some extensions require third-party access tokens (Slack, Notion, etc.). Developer-provided README files explain token acquisition processes, though not all extensions include this documentation.

content.content:
# Visual Details of the Detail View

## Layout Structure
The Detail component employs a **split-view layout** with markdown content on the left and an optional metadata panel on the right side. "Renders a markdown (CommonMark) string with an optional metadata panel," typically deployed as a standalone view or following list navigation.

## Markdown Rendering
- Supports CommonMark formatting for text styling and structure
- Displays images from asset directories with customizable dimensions via query parameters (`raycast-width`, `raycast-height`)
- Applies tint colors to images using the `raycast-tintColor` parameter
- Renders LaTeX mathematical expressions with support for inline math (`\(...\)`, `\begin{math}...\end{math}`) and display math (`\[...\]`, `$$...$$`, `\begin{equation}...\end{equation}`)

## Metadata Panel Components
The right-side metadata area includes:

- **Label items**: Display title-text pairs with optional icons
- **Links**: Clickable items with title, display text, and target URL
- **TagList**: Rows of colored tags with optional icons and click handlers
- **Separator**: Visual dividing lines for grouping metadata sections

## Visual Properties
- Navigation title displayed at the top in Raycast
- Optional loading bar below the search interface
- Color customization for text labels and tag backgrounds
- Icon support throughout metadata elements for visual enhancement

# Form Components: Comprehensive Details

## Form Field Types

The Raycast Form supports these input components:

**Text Input Fields:**
- **TextField**: Standard text input with optional placeholder and character entry
- **PasswordField**: "A form item with a secure text field for password-entry in which the entered characters must be kept secret"
- **TextArea**: Multiline text entry supporting optional markdown shortcuts via `enableMarkdown` prop

**Selection Components:**
- **Checkbox**: Boolean toggle with label displayed on the right side
- **Dropdown**: Menu selection with optional section grouping and filtering
- **TagPicker**: "A form item with a tag picker that allows the user to select multiple items"
- **DatePicker**: Date/time selection supporting full-day or specific time modes via `type` prop

**File Management:**
- **FilePicker**: File/directory selection supporting single or multiple selection modes

**Layout:**
- **Separator**: Visual divider for grouping related fields

## Visual & Structural Details

**Labels & Display:**
- Text fields show titles on the left side
- Checkboxes display labels on the right
- Optional `info` messages appear with hover-activated icons
- Error messages display on the right with red border highlighting

**Placeholders:**
Fields support `placeholder` props for input hints (e.g., "Tim Cook" for name field).

**Validation:**
The documentation emphasizes: "Before submitting data, it is important to ensure all required form controls are filled out, in the correct format."

Validation occurs via `onBlur` callbacks that update item `error` properties. The `useForm` hook provides recommended validation handling.

**Form-Level Props:**
- `enableDrafts`: Preserves unfilled values when exiting
- `isLoading`: Loading indicator display
- `navigationTitle`: Form heading
- `searchBarAccessory`: Right-aligned toolbar element

## Control Types

Forms support **controlled** (explicit value management) and **uncontrolled** (React-managed) implementations via `value`/`onChange` or `defaultValue` patterns respectively.

# Grid View Visual Details

## Column Layout & Sizing
The Grid component uses a configurable column system with **1-8 columns maximum**. Individual sections can override the main grid's column count. The deprecated `Grid.ItemSize` enum previously controlled columns (Small: 8 items/row, Medium: 5 items/row, Large: 3 items/row).

## Item Dimensions & Aspect Ratios
Grid items support multiple aspect ratio options: `"1"` (square), `"3/2"`, `"2/3"`, `"4/3"`, `"3/4"`, `"16/9"`, and `"9/16"`. The default is square (1:1 ratio).

## Content Display
Items showcase images, emojis, or color blocks as their primary visual element. The `content` prop is mandatory and accepts `Image.ImageLike` objects, color definitions, or objects with tooltips and values. Two fit modes control display: **Contain** (preserves aspect ratio with bars) and **Fill** (scales to fill cell, potentially cropping).

## Spacing & Insets
Three inset levels control padding between item content and borders: Small, Medium, and Large. The absolute pixel values scale based on the column count configuration.

## Item Metadata
Grid items display optional titles below content and subtitles below titles. Keywords enable search filtering. Items support Quick Look previews for file access.

## Accessories & Actions
"Grid.Item does not *currently* support accessories" beyond the basic accessory slot. ActionPanels attach contextual actions to individual items, appearing on selection.

I've found search results related to your query about macOS app window vibrancy and NSVisualEffectView. Here's what the search returned:

## Key Findings:

**NSVisualEffectView Basics:**
NSVisualEffectView is a view that adds translucency and vibrancy effects to the views in your interface.

**Translucency vs. Vibrancy:**
These are related but distinct concepts. Translucency and the blurring of background content adds depth to your interface, while vibrancy is a subtle blending of foreground and background colors to increase the contrast and make the foreground content stand out visually.

**Material Property:**
Material is associated with translucency, or how the contents behind your UI are blurred and blended to provide the desired background effect, and NSVisualEffectView's material really determines the background—how the contents behind it affect your UI.

**Using NSVisualEffectView for Window Effects:**
NSVisualEffectView is an official API for implementing blur effects and, by traversing the layers, you can remove the background color that comes with the view, avoiding private APIs and aligning better with macOS's native design.

**Content Positioning Matters:**
Content overlaid over NSVisualEffectView shows no vibrancy, while content added to the view hierarchy of an NSVisualEffectView shows vibrant appearance.

The search results provide technical documentation and practical guidance for implementing translucent, blurred window backgrounds in macOS applications.

content.content:
# Raycast 1.38.0 UI Redesign Visual Details

## Search Bar
The search bar was enlarged to approximate Spotlight's dimensions, creating better visual distinction from search results below it.

## Action Bar
A new bottom panel displays the primary executable command alongside an actions button. Clicking this button reveals a popup menu with context-specific options (e.g., "opening the file, show in finder, quick look, open with" for file searches). As one user noted, "The Action Bar is a nifty addition, but there is no option to toggle the bar."

## Compact Mode
This view presents only the search bar initially without the shortcuts list visible. Upon typing, "the interface expands to its normal view, i.e., to display the relevant results." This allows users to see content beneath the launcher before querying.

## Icons
Raycast introduced a new application icon resembling a key cap, reflecting its keystroke launcher function. The icon set throughout the application underwent redesign alongside this change.

## Overall Assessment
The redesign maintains core functionality while introducing "subtle changes" that enhance accessibility and visual hierarchy, particularly for users who previously struggled accessing commands.

# Raycast Theme Structure Analysis

Based on the GitHub repository content provided, I found limited explicit theme structure details. However, here's what's documented:

## Theme Contribution Process

The repository indicates themes are managed through JSON files. According to the README:

> "In [themes](/raycast/ray-so/blob/main/app/(navigation)/themes/themes), create a folder with your Raycast username, ie: `peduarte`. In that folder, create a file with the theme name, ie: `red.json`"

## Theme Export Method

Raycast users can extract their theme configuration by:

> "Open Theme Studio in Raycast. Right click on your Theme and select 'Copy as JSON'"

## Key Observation

The documentation describes the **process for contributing themes** but does not expose the actual color property names or detailed structure schema within this page content. The specific Raycast theme color definitions (like property naming conventions, required fields, or color value formats) are not detailed in the provided web page excerpt.

To access the complete theme structure definition, you would need to examine the actual theme JSON files stored in the `/app/(navigation)/themes/themes/` directory or review Raycast's official theme documentation.

# Comprehensive Raycast UI Research Report

## 1. Main Command Palette Window

### Window Dimensions and Structure
From Raycast's official `ray-so` theme preview source code (`/app/(navigation)/themes/components/raycast/index.tsx`):

- **Window size**: `750px` wide x `475px` tall
- **Border radius**: `18px` (rounded-[18px])
- **Backdrop blur**: `72px` (`backdrop-blur-[72px]`)
- **Background**: Semi-transparent with gradient overlay -- dark mode uses `rgba(128, 128, 128, 0.2)` as a base, then applies a linear gradient from `backgroundPrimary` (40% alpha) to `backgroundSecondary` (40% alpha) from top to bottom
- **Border**: `inset 0 0 0 1px` using text color at 20% opacity
- **Window shadow**: Large drop shadow (defined via Tailwind `shadow` class)
- **The window uses macOS `NSVisualEffectView` vibrancy/translucency** for the native app, meaning the background blurs desktop content behind it

### Search Bar (Header)
From `root-header.tsx`:

- **Height**: `56px` (`h-[56px]`)
- **Padding**: `16px` horizontal (`px-4`)
- **Bottom border**: `1px` solid, using text color at 10% opacity (`border-[--border-100]`)
- **Layout**: Flex row with `gap-2` (8px)
- **Back button**: `24x24px` rounded-md square with text-100 background, containing a 16x16 left-arrow SVG icon (stroke-based, 1.5px stroke width, round linecap/linejoin)
- **Search text**: `text-base` (16px), text color at 40% opacity, displays placeholder "Search for apps and commands..."
- **Loading bar**: Sits at the absolute bottom of the header, `200px` wide, `1px` tall, with a gradient from transparent to `--loader` color back to transparent, animated with a `nightRider` sweep animation (2s ease-in-out infinite)

### List Items
From `list.tsx`:

- **Section header**: `text-xs` (12px), `leading-none`, `font-medium`, `tracking-[0.1px]`, text at 60% opacity, with `padding: 8px`
- **List item height**: `40px` (`h-[40px]`)
- **Item padding**: `8px` horizontal (`px-2`)
- **Item border radius**: `10px` (`rounded-[10px]`)
- **Selected item background**: `--selection-100` (selection color at 10% opacity)
- **Item layout**: Flex row with `gap-3` (12px) between icon and text
- **Item icon**: `20x20px` (`w-5 h-5`)
- **Item text**: `text-sm` (14px), `leading-none`
- **Accessory tags**: `22px` tall, `text-sm`, `px-2`, `rounded-md` (6px), each with its own color and background at 15% alpha
- **Container**: `py-1 px-2` (4px vertical, 8px horizontal padding)

### Action Bar (Footer)
From `root-footer.tsx`:

- **Height**: `40px` (`h-[40px]`)
- **Padding**: `12px` horizontal (`px-3`)
- **Top border**: `1px` solid at text 10% opacity
- **Background**: `bg-white/5` in dark mode, `bg-white/20` in light mode
- **Left side**: Raycast logo icon (20x20px) + "Theme Explorer" label in `text-sm` at 60% text opacity
- **Right side**: Primary action label ("Open Command") in `text-xs font-semibold` full text color + Enter key badge (22x20px, rounded-md, text-100 bg) | Separator (2x12px at text-100) | "Actions" label in `text-xs font-semibold` at 60% opacity + two key badges for Cmd+K (24x20px each, -8px overlap)
- **Key badges**: `rounded-md`, `bg-[--text-100]`, `text-[--text]`

### Grid View
From `grid.tsx`:

- **Grid columns**: 5 columns (`grid-cols-5`)
- **Grid gap**: `8px` (`gap-2`)
- **Grid item image**: `138x138px`, `rounded-lg` (8px)
- **Selected item**: `inset 0 0 0 2px var(--selection)` box-shadow
- **Grid item title**: `text-sm` (14px)
- **Grid item subtitle**: `text-xs` (12px), 60% text opacity
- **Section header**: Same as list sections

---

## 2. Raycast Theme Color System

### Theme Structure (12 Color Tokens)
From `theme.ts` in `ray-so`:

```typescript
type Theme = {
  author: string;
  authorUsername: string;
  version: string;
  name: string;
  appearance: "light" | "dark";
  colors: {
    background: string;       // Primary background
    backgroundSecondary: string; // Secondary/gradient end background
    text: string;             // Primary text color
    selection: string;        // Selected item highlight
    loader: string;           // Loading bar color
    red: string;
    orange: string;
    yellow: string;
    green: string;
    blue: string;
    purple: string;
    magenta: string;
  };
};
```

### Derived CSS Variables (from `index.tsx`)
Each color generates multiple alpha variants:

| Variable | Value |
|----------|-------|
| `--backgroundPrimary` | `background` + 40% alpha (hex `66`) |
| `--backgroundSecondary` | `backgroundSecondary` + 40% alpha |
| `--border-100` | `text` + 10% alpha (hex `1A`) |
| `--border-200` | `text` + 20% alpha (hex `33`) |
| `--text` | `text` at full opacity |
| `--text-100` | `text` + 10% alpha |
| `--text-400` | `text` + 40% alpha |
| `--text-600` | `text` + 60% alpha |
| `--selection` | `selection` at full opacity |
| `--selection-100` | `selection` + 10% alpha |
| `--[color]` | Each accent color at full opacity |
| `--[color]-100` | Each accent color at 15% alpha (hex `26`) |

### Alpha Hex Suffixes
From `alpha.ts`:

| Opacity | Hex Suffix |
|---------|-----------|
| 5% | `0D` |
| 10% | `1A` |
| 15% | `26` |
| 20% | `33` |
| 40% | `66` |
| 60% | `99` |
| 80% | `CC` |
| 100% | `FF` |

### Default Dark Theme Approximation
From the custom themes manual, the dark background default is approximately `#191919` with text at `#e1e1e1`. The project tokens file already uses `#191919` for background.

---

## 3. Typography

- **Font family**: [Inter](https://rsms.me/work/inter/) with custom letter and line spacing, confirmed by [Raycast's official statement](https://twitter.com/raycastapp/status/1300441003137040389). Falls back to system font stack: `-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'SF Pro Text'`
- **Adjustable sizes**: Users can choose between default and larger text size in Preferences > Appearance
- **Size scale observed in ray-so source**: `text-xs` (12px), `text-sm` (14px), `text-base` (16px)
- **Font weights**: Regular (400) for body text, `font-medium` (500) for section headers, `font-semibold` (600) for action labels and key badges
- **Letter spacing**: `tracking-[0.1px]` on section headers
- **Line height**: `leading-none` (1.0) used extensively in list items and headers for tight, compact layout

---

## 4. All Screens and Views

### Root Search (Main Command Palette)
The default view when invoked via hotkey (Alt+Space default). Shows search bar + list of recently used commands, applications, and suggestions.

### List View
Multiple similar items in a scrollable list with sections, items (icon + title + subtitle + accessories), and an action panel footer. Items can show detail panels (split view, markdown left + metadata right).

### Grid View
Image-heavy layout with 1-8 configurable columns. Items display images/icons with title and subtitle below. Supports aspect ratios: `1:1`, `3:2`, `2:3`, `4:3`, `3:4`, `16:9`, `9:16`.

### Detail View
Split layout: Markdown content (CommonMark with LaTeX support) on the left + optional metadata panel on the right. Metadata includes Labels, Links, TagLists, and Separators.

### Form View
Input form with field types: TextField, PasswordField, TextArea, Checkbox, Dropdown, TagPicker, DatePicker, FilePicker, Separator. Labels on the left, inputs on the right, error messages with red borders.

### Settings / Preferences Window
Accessed via Cmd+comma. Tabs: **General** (launch at login, Spotlight replacement), **Appearance** (light/dark/system theme, text size), **Extensions** (enable/disable, aliases, hotkeys), **Advanced** (display position, timeout, import/export), **About** (version info).

### Extension Store
Browsable/searchable catalog of extensions. Search by name or filter by category (Ctrl+P). Extension detail view shows commands, developer info, metadata. Enter to install, Cmd+Enter for quick install.

### AI Chat
Standalone resizable floating window. Conversational layout with message bubbles, model selector (32+ models), Cmd+K command menu, file/image/browser-tab attachments, code blocks with copy button and syntax highlighting, thumbs up/down feedback on hover, chat branching (Cmd+Shift+B experimental).

### Raycast Notes
Floating note-taking window with full Markdown support (headings, bold/italic/strikethrough, code blocks, lists, checklists, links). Cmd+K action panel, Cmd+N for new notes, Cmd+P to search notes. Export to plain text, markdown, or HTML.

### Clipboard History
Searchable history of copied text, images, files, links, and colors. Filterable by type and date.

### Calculator
Inline calculation from the search bar. Supports math expressions, unit conversion, timezone conversion, date math.

### Emoji Picker
Triggered by typing `:` in text areas. Grid of emoji with search.

### Snippets
Text expansion with dynamic placeholders. Manages frequently used text blocks.

### Quicklinks
URL/file/folder shortcuts with optional query parameters. Launchable from root search.

### Window Management
Grid-based window positioning and sizing. Supports multiple monitors.

### Confetti
Full-screen confetti animation triggered by command.

---

## 5. UI Components

### Action Panel (Cmd+K)
- Opens as a popover/dropdown from the action bar
- Contains `ActionPanel.Section` groups with titles
- Each action has: icon + label + keyboard shortcut badge
- Supports `ActionPanel.Submenu` for hierarchical navigation with search/filter
- Primary action: Enter, Secondary: Cmd+Enter
- Form primary: Cmd+Enter, Form secondary: Cmd+Shift+Enter

### Search Bar
- Large input with placeholder text
- Optional back navigation button (left arrow)
- Optional dropdown accessory on the right side (Cmd+P to activate)
- Loading indicator bar beneath

### Loading Bar
- Thin gradient line (1px height) beneath search bar
- Gradient: transparent -> loader color -> transparent
- Animation: sweeps back and forth (`nightRider` keyframes, 2s ease-in-out infinite)

### Tags / Accessories
- Colored pills/badges on list items (right-aligned)
- Each tag: color text on 15% alpha background of same color
- Rounded-md (6px), px-2 padding, 22px height

### Key Badges (Kbd)
- Background: text at 10% opacity
- Border radius: `rounded-md` (6px)
- Sizes: 22x20px for single chars, 24x20px for symbols
- Font: `text-xs`

### Toast Notifications
- Appear in the left side of the action bar
- Show success/failure/loading states
- Toast.Style: Animated, Success, Failure

### Empty View
- Centered icon + title + description
- Supports custom action panel
- Raycast provides a default empty view

### Dropdown (Search Bar Accessory)
- Right-side dropdown on search bar
- Sections with items
- Keyboard trigger: Cmd+P
- Supports value persistence

### Metadata Panel (Detail/List Detail)
- Label: title + optional icon + text
- Link: clickable URL with display text
- TagList: row of colored tags with click handlers
- Separator: horizontal divider line

---

## 6. Micro-Interactions and Animations

1. **Loading bar sweep**: Gradient light sweeps horizontally beneath the search bar, 2s cycle, ease-in-out
2. **Window appearance**: Fade-in on invocation
3. **List item selection**: Background highlight with selection color at 10% alpha
4. **Compact mode**: Window starts as search-bar-only, expands when typing
5. **Action panel open/close**: Popover animation
6. **Toast transitions**: Slide/fade for notifications
7. **Confetti**: Full-screen particle animation
8. **Grid item selection**: 2px inset border in selection color
9. **Theme transitions**: Smooth color scheme changes
10. **Design principles**: "fast, simple, and delightful" -- all animations are fast and subtle

---

## 7. Exact Color Scheme (Default Dark Theme)

Based on cross-referencing the custom themes manual (`#191919` bg, `#e1e1e1` text), the project's existing tokens, and the theme system:

| Token | Purpose | Approximate Value |
|-------|---------|------------------|
| `background` | Main window bg | `#191919` |
| `backgroundSecondary` | Gradient end bg | `#232325` (slightly lighter) |
| `text` | Primary text | `#E5E5EA` (near-white) |
| `selection` | Selected item color | `#007AFF` (Apple blue) |
| `loader` | Loading bar color | `#007AFF` |
| `red` | Semantic red | `#FF453A` |
| `orange` | Semantic orange | `#FF9F0A` |
| `yellow` | Semantic yellow | `#FFD60A` |
| `green` | Semantic green | `#30D158` |
| `blue` | Semantic blue | `#007AFF` |
| `purple` | Semantic purple | `#BF5AF2` |
| `magenta` | Semantic magenta | `#FF375F` |

Secondary text uses `text` at 60% alpha. Tertiary text at 40% alpha. Borders use `text` at 10-20% alpha. Backgrounds are layered with vibrancy/translucency.

---

## 8. Font Choices

- **Primary**: Inter (custom letter-spacing and line-spacing)
- **Fallback stack**: `-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'SF Pro Text', 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`
- **Monospace**: `'SF Mono', SFMono-Regular, ui-monospace, Menlo, monospace`

---

## 9. Spacing System

From the ray-so source, Tailwind classes correspond to:
- `gap-2`: 8px
- `gap-3`: 12px
- `px-2`: 8px
- `px-3`: 12px
- `px-4`: 16px
- `py-1`: 4px
- `p-2`: 8px

The project's existing `_tokens.scss` uses a 4px base grid (4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32px).

---

## 10. Other Distinctive UI Patterns

- **Window is centered horizontally** on screen, positioned in the upper third
- **No title bar** -- the window is chromeless with just content
- **The back button** in the search bar appears only during navigation (not at root)
- **Icons throughout use outline style** with consistent stroke width and uniform corner radii (redesigned by James McDonald)
- **macOS vibrancy**: The window uses `NSVisualEffectView` for native translucency, meaning desktop content bleeds through the semi-transparent background
- **Compact Mode**: Shows only the search bar until the user starts typing, then expands to show results
- **All interactions are keyboard-first**: Every action has a discoverable shortcut shown in the action bar
